title: MCP CRUD — mcp-writer.ts (add/update/remove) + reusable QuartersDrawer/ConfirmDialog shell + MCP transport form + detail endpoint; MCPs panel Add/Edit/Remove

tests.summary: 13 passed — mcp-writer (10): add to missing file (wrapped), refuse clobber, preserve BARE map shape, preserve sibling keys under wrapped shape, http/sse round-trip, update+rename, refuse rename collision, remove + not-found, name/transport validation, normalizeServerConfig cross-transport drop + bespoke keep. quarters dispatch (3): add→detail→update→remove round-trip, CrudResult error surfaced not thrown, unknown action rejected.

suite.summary: 406 passed, 2 skipped, 0 failed across the whole suite minus the env-dependent live-gateway test.

typecheck.summary: 0 errors (mcp-writer + quarters-detail + quarters dispatch widened to CrudResult + 4 new UI components)

build.summary: /quarters/[type] built at 19.1 kB; all routes compiled

e2e.summary: 1 passed — add → edit → remove an MCP server with a UNIQUE fixture name (ctx7-e2e); seeded sandbox-mcp untouched; self-contained, no suite-order dependence, no residue

by: vision screenshots c1-mcps-list.png + c1-mcps-add-form.png vs Vault/Armory/shell
designAudit.notes: Add-server button top-right; row shows LOOSE pill + Edit (pencil) + Remove (trash, alarm-red); form drawer on the existing .scrim overlay with mono labels, paper/rule fields, transport switch, Cancel/Add footer; matches the shell visual language; no emoji; no new idiom

video.note: Per-slice walkthrough deferred to a consolidated 'manage everything from Quarters' walkthrough at the global gate (turn-budget + recorder-serialization). Re-runnable committed gate is the e2e round-trip + unit specs.

notes: The headline ask ('we can't even remove an mcp!'). mcp-writer.ts is Garrison's writer-of-record for ~/.claude/mcp.json: preserves the file's wrapper shape ({mcpServers:{}} vs bare map) + sibling keys, round-trips both transports (stdio command/args/env + http/sse url/headers), validates name+transport, supports rename. Establishes the reusable CRUD shell (QuartersDrawer + ConfirmDialog on .scrim) and the surfaceEditors registry + CrudResult contract that C2–C5 inherit. The 2 suite failures are tests/orchestrator-integration.test.ts hitting a stray live Garrison gateway (PID on :4777, the user's own running operative) that defeats the test's skip-by-default — environmental, unrelated to Quarters, not killed.
